_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack surfaces, threat actors, and applicable mitigating controls.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI RM domain. Task RM-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-005 (RM, cosine: 0.569), RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.543).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.9/10 (raw 0.387). Shared vocabulary: [threat]. NICE-only terms [defense, informed, knowledge] vs WIDAI-only terms [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, conduct] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.883, R=0.855). P exceeds R by 0.029 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Knowledge of threat-informed defense" and "Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.608 > STS 0.387 (gap +0.221). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [threat] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.0%. Shared tokens: [threat]. NICE-only: [defense, informed, knowledge]. WIDAI-only: [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, conduct].
